name: Bar Reef Marine Sanctuary
description: One of Sri Lanka’s largest coral reefs, vital for biodiversity. Fishing restricted to protect reef species.

name: Hikkaduwa Marine National Park
description: Coral sanctuary and popular tourist reef. Fishing and anchoring prohibited inside park boundaries.

name: Pigeon Island National Park
description: Located off Nilaveli coast; nesting grounds for rock pigeons and coral habitat for reef fish.

name: Kayankerni Reef Marine Sanctuary
description: Coral reef ecosystem declared as sanctuary in 2011. Coral collection and destructive fishing banned.

name: Rumassala Marine Sanctuary
description: Marine habitat near Unawatuna famous for coral and mythical Hanuman hill. Fishing restricted.

name: Mirissa Marine Protected Area
description: Proposed protected area to safeguard whale and dolphin habitats in the southern coast.

name: Adam's Bridge National Park
description: Marine National Park protecting seagrass beds and sand shoals linking Sri Lanka and India. Established in 2015.[1]

name: Kuda Ravana Maha Ravana Sanctuary
description: Offshore marine sanctuary protecting the Great Basses and Little Basses reefs, vital for deep coral systems and pelagic species. Declared in 2019.[5, 2]

name: Great Sober Island Sanctuary
description: Coastal island sanctuary within Trincomalee Bay, historically a military post and now protecting coastal island ecosystems. Established in 1963.[5, 3]

name: Little Sober Island Sanctuary
description: Small island sanctuary adjacent to Great Sober Island in Trincomalee Bay, part of the 1963 sanctuary declaration.[5, 6]

name: Muthurajawela Sanctuary
description: Wetland sanctuary protecting Sri Lanka’s largest saline coastal peat bog, integrated with the Negombo lagoon ecosystem. Declared in 1996.[4, 7]

name: Nayaru Nature Reserve
description: Nature Reserve established in 2017 to protect a lagoon, wetland, and mangroves on the East Coast, providing habitat for migratory birds. Contains approximately 25% marine habitats.[5]

name: Nandikadal Nature Reserve
description: Nature Reserve protecting a lagoon ecosystem, known as a critical feeding and roosting ground for migratory and resident birds. Established in 2017.[5]

name: Parititivu Island Sanctuary
description: Island Sanctuary (97.1 ha) established in 1973 protecting fringing coral reefs, seagrass, and subtidal reefs off the Jaffna Peninsula.[5]

name: Wedithalathivu Nature Reserve
description: Nature Reserve on the west coast comprising brackish and marine habitats, including corals, mangroves, and seagrass meadows.[5, 8]
